ddls:
CREATE TABLE "majors" (
  "major_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each major */,
  "major_code" INTEGER /* Code assigned to the major */,
  "major_name" TEXT /* Name of the major */,
  "major_category" TEXT /* Category of the major (e.g., Engineering) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the major */,
  "degree_level" TEXT /* Degree level (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, Ph.D.) */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Computer Science) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("major_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_majors_major_category FOREIGN KEY ("major_category") REFERENCES major_categories ("category_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "major_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Engineering) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category" TEXT /* Parent category (e.g., STEM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "graduates" (
  "graduate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each graduate record */,
  "major_id" INTEGER /* ID of the major */,
  "total_graduates" INTEGER /* Total number of graduates for the major */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Sample size of graduates surveyed */,
  "men" INTEGER /* Number of male graduates */,
  "women" INTEGER /* Number of female graduates */,
  "share_women" REAL /* Percentage of female graduates */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of graduation */,
  "degree_level" TEXT /* Degree level (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, Ph.D.) */,
  "full_time_enrollment" INTEGER /* Number of graduates enrolled full-time */,
  "part_time_enrollment" INTEGER /* Number of graduates enrolled part-time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("graduate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduates_major_id FOREIGN KEY ("major_id") REFERENCES majors ("major_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduates_year FOREIGN KEY ("year") REFERENCES years ("year")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment status record */,
  "graduate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the graduate record */,
  "employed" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed */,
  "full_time" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed full-time */,
  "part_time" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed part-time */,
  "full_time_year_round" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed full-time year-round */,
  "unemployed" INTEGER /* Number of graduates unemployed */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate for the major */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title (e.g., Software Engineer, Data Scientist) */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry (e.g., Technology, Finance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_status_graduate_id FOREIGN KEY ("graduate_id") REFERENCES graduates ("graduate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_details" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary detail record */,
  "graduate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the graduate record */,
  "median_salary" INTEGER /* Median salary for graduates */,
  "percentile_25" INTEGER /* 25th percentile salary */,
  "percentile_75" INTEGER /* 75th percentile salary */,
  "college_jobs" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed in college-level jobs */,
  "non_college_jobs" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed in non-college-level jobs */,
  "low_wage_jobs" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed in low-wage jobs */,
  "average_salary" INTEGER /* Average salary for graduates */,
  "highest_salary" INTEGER /* Highest salary for graduates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_details_graduate_id FOREIGN KEY ("graduate_id") REFERENCES graduates ("graduate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of data collection */,
  "academic_year" TEXT /* Academic year (e.g., 2022-2023) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Northeast, South) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_by_region" (
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment record */,
  "graduate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the graduate record */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "employed" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed in the region */,
  "unemployed" INTEGER /* Number of graduates unemployed in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_by_region_graduate_id FOREIGN KEY ("graduate_id") REFERENCES graduates ("graduate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_by_region_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)
column_names: Field of study (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Computer Science)
